exactLayouts.MHI2: 464-byte node records: links, 256-byte name, signed mesh-data index, initial world/local 4x4 matrices, retained tail hash
exactLayouts.MAMD: 124-byte header; 32-byte position/normal/UV vertices; uint16 triangle indices; uint32 material-per-face list; uint32 source-vertex remap
exactLayouts.MANI: 24-byte counted header; float32 position/quaternion/scale key arrays

decoder.coordinateBoundary: Positions, matrices and quaternions remain in exact TV3D source order. No browser handedness or matrix convention conversion is asserted.
decoder.materialBoundary: MAG3 is retained by size/hash and every decoded MAMD face uses embedded material index 0. Runtime appearance is source-backed by the stronger CLCubX StdMat override.

Host plays the separately authored Get actor and later swaps to CubXOpen.

Host plays the separately authored Get actor and later swaps to CubXOpen.
Source indexes the uninitialized eighth actor slot; the render loop also stops at slot 6.

openingLineage.interpretation: The three panel payloads are byte-identical after the MAMD header, and their first 51 animation keys differ by at most float32 export noise. However the full actor replaces Box05 while the solo source names the same panel lineage Box08, so BoxNN is not a stable semantic identifier across these exports.

cubXEvidence.fact: Actor material ambient is explicitly overridden.
Actor material diffuse is explicitly overridden.
Actor material specular is explicitly overridden.
Actor material power is explicitly overridden.
Closed mesh is loaded from the missing Media/CubX directory name.
Closed mesh uses the constructor position.
Constructor Size scaling is commented out.
The full open actor is the only open TVA loaded by CLCubX.
Full open actor receives the source StdMat override.
Only seven rotation actor slots are initialized.
Every initialized Get actor receives the source StdMat override.
Eight click meshes are loaded from the missing Media/CubX directory name.
Selection directly indexes selectedCube-1 and plays forward.
Selection waits for the authored frame-100 terminal hold instead of IsAnimationFinished.
cubXEvidence.code: if(CubXRotActor[iSelectedCube-1]->GetKeyFrame()    >  99)    //   PKOI le Is Finished Marche pas???????

cubXEvidence.fact: Opening changes to OPENED only after the full 0..100 range.
Both EnableCubXButtons and the misnamed DisableCubXButtons enable click meshes.

sceneEvidence.fact: Scene constructs CLCubX at the exact host position; the Size argument is ignored by commented scaling.
sceneEvidence.code: clCubX = new CLCubX(4, NULL, cTV_3DVECTOR(-400,500,0), true);

sceneEvidence.fact: Click mesh 1 enters the explicit selection chain.
Click mesh 8 is accepted despite the missing actor slot.

alternateClickEvidence.fact: The alternate direct Area host repeats click mesh 1 selection.
The alternate direct Area host also accepts click mesh 8.

defects: Seven actor slots are initialized from Get2..Get8, but eight click indices are accepted and selection 8 indexes uninitialized slot 7.
The render loop covers only slots 0..6.
DisableCubXButtons enables the click meshes instead of disabling them.
CLOSING, DESELECTING, FOCUSED, OPENED and DEFOCUSING contain no transition behavior; the internal menu registration block is commented out.
Hard-coded Media/CubX asset paths do not exist in the surviving tree; the files live under Media/CubXActor.

exactNewEvidence: Click mesh 1..8 local centers and their spatial overlap with the eight closed BoxNN cells.
Click index 1..7 selects Get2..Get8; click 8 selects an uninitialized actor slot.
Get2..8 and Rot1..7 each animate only Group01; CubXOpen animates Right/Top/Left; CubeOpensolo animates Right08/Top08/Left08.
CubXOpen omits closed Box05 and supplies Cube/Right/Top/Left, while CubeOpensolo calls its homologous panel lineage Box08/Right08/Top08/Left08.
mappingAssessment.whyNotResolved: The click meshes spatially map to Box05, Box04, Box03, Box02, Box08, Box06, Box07, Box01, but the host maps click indices 1..7 to filenames Get2..Get8 and has no valid click-8 actor. The full and solo opening exports also disagree between Box05 and Box08 naming. Those contradictions prevent a defensible semantic click-index-to-BoxNN mapping for CubZ.
mappingAssessment.safeReuse: Use exact geometry, transforms, timing and per-file animation data only after repairing path/slot defects. Keep click index, spatial BoxNN, actor filename, and opening-assembly label as separate fields rather than collapsing them into one invented mapping.

exact: All hashes, chunk counts, hierarchy names/links/mesh indices/matrices, MAMD geometry summaries, 30 fps ranges and MANI keys.
Companion CubXMesh/CubXBtn bounds and exact source host calls/material/position constants.
The source defects and the separate unused Rot/CubeOpensolo asset families.
derived: Button-to-BoxNN spatial association is nearest exact local-center overlap with less than one source unit of export drift.
Rotation-prototype pairing follows the exact RotN/GetN+1 filenames and near-equal terminal quaternions.
Motion-complete time is the first key of the terminal quaternion plateau; the remaining authored frames are a terminal hold.
notClaimed: TV3D-to-web handedness, quaternion application order, engine interpolation or visual actor-swap continuity.
That a BoxNN modeling label equals a user-facing click number or CubZ selection number.
That unreferenced CubXRot/CubeOpensolo assets were executed by the surviving CLCubX host.
